hops.context: An oriented tree T is δ-enforcible if every G with δ (G) ≥ d contains T as a subgraph.
H¨aggkvist conjecture [8] states that every digraph has a directed cycle of length at most ⌈n/d⌉.
H¨aggkvist conjecture [8] states that every digraph has a directed cycle of length at most ⌈n/d⌉.

question: Which of the following statements accurately describes the enforcement of an oriented tree within the context of graph theory?

options.A: An oriented tree is δ-enforcible only in digraphs that do not contain any directed cycles as graph components.
options.B: An oriented tree is δ-enforcible if it itself contains a directed cycle as a component in all enforcing digraphs.
options.C: An oriented tree is δ-enforcible if every graph containing it as a subgraph has a directed cycle with a length of at most ⌈n/d⌉.
options.D: An oriented tree is δ-enforcible if it can be found as a subgraph in every digraph that contains a directed cycle as a graph component.

explanation: The correct answer is A because the oriented tree is defined as δ-enforcible if it appears as a subgraph in every graph that meets certain criteria (in this case, every digraph). The digraph, which enforces the oriented tree, contains a directed cycle as a graph component. Hence, the oriented tree needs to be represented in these specific conditions, making option A correct. Options B, C, and D introduce incorrect conditions or relationships not supported by the given knowledge path or the context from the paper.
context: An oriented tree T is δ-enforcible if every G with δ (G) ≥ d contains T as a subgraph. | H¨aggkvist conjecture [8] states that every digraph has a directed cycle of length at most ⌈n/d⌉. | H¨aggkvist conjecture [8] states that every digraph has a directed cycle of length at most ⌈n/d⌉.

question: In the context of constructing Gk,d as described in the paper, which statement accurately reflects the relationship and composition of Gk,d?

options.A: Gk,d consists of a single copy of Bk,d, enhanced by adding additional leaves connected to existing roots.
options.B: Gk,d involves taking d disjoint copies of Bk,d and connecting every leaf in a copy to every root within the same copy.
options.C: Gk,d is constructed by taking multiple copies of Bk,d, each being interconnected at their respective roots only.
options.D: Gk,d is formed by taking d disjoint copies of Bk,d and connecting each leaf of one copy to every root of all other copies.

explanation: The correct construction of Gk,d, as mentioned in the text, involves taking d disjoint copies of Bk,d and adding edges from every leaf in each copy to every root within the same copy, and not between different copies or only connecting roots.
